decisions.decision: Use ports/adapters architecture for auth and ranking.
decisions.reason: Firebase is MVP infrastructure, not product architecture.
decisions.consequence: UI, gameplay, timer and ranking must depend on AuthService, RankingService, AuthPort and RankingPort.

decisions.decision: Place Firebase Auth and Firestore adapters under infrastructure/firebase only.
decisions.reason: Allow later replacement by corporate, API, SSO, SQL or backend providers.
decisions.consequence: No Firebase SDK imports outside infrastructure adapters.

decisions.decision: Measure completion time in frontend for MVP.
decisions.reason: Fast MVP delivery with simple gameplay loop.
decisions.consequence: Users with technical knowledge can manipulate time; production needs server-side validation.

decisions.decision: Centralize premium visual language in design tokens.
decisions.reason: Prevent color drift and keep brand consistency across screens.
decisions.consequence: Components must use tokens instead of hardcoded loose colors.

decisions.decision: Build mobile-first with centered elegant desktop presentation.
decisions.reason: Game interaction is naturally mobile-like while still needing desktop polish.
decisions.consequence: Screens must work full-screen on mobile and avoid awkward stretching on desktop.

decisions.decision: Use React + TypeScript + Vite for the Supernova web app scaffold.
decisions.reason: The repository had no framework and Vite is the simplest fit for this small responsive game.
decisions.consequence: App entrypoint lives in src/main.tsx and screens are composed from src/App.tsx.

decisions.decision: Use src/providers/appProviders.ts as the provider composition root.
decisions.reason: Auth and ranking providers must be replaceable without changing screens or hooks.
decisions.consequence: Provider swaps happen in appProviders.ts; screens keep consuming hooks and services.

decisions.decision: Auth and ranking providers implement stable internal ports; appProviders selects firebase or mock providers.
decisions.reason: Screens and hooks must remain provider-agnostic while Firebase stays the initial adapter.
decisions.consequence: Provider replacement requires changing only src/providers/appProviders.ts and adding a port-compatible adapter.

decisions.decision: Firebase Auth uses Google sign-in and Firestore stores ranking results in the gameResults collection.
decisions.reason: Firebase is the MVP provider for login and ranking while the app depends on internal ports.
decisions.consequence: Firestore documents use the GameResult fields; Firebase SDK imports remain isolated to src/infrastructure/firebase.

decisions.decision: Implement merge gameplay with provider-free hooks and Pointer Events drag/drop over gameContent pairs.
decisions.reason: The game must work with mouse and touch while keeping match logic testable outside UI.
decisions.consequence: useMergeGame owns merge state and pure mergeRules; GameScreen only renders interaction and completion flow.

decisions.decision: Save completed game results through RankingService from a one-shot useSaveGameResult hook and show an in-game completion panel.
decisions.reason: Completed games must persist ranking data without coupling UI to Firebase and without double submits.
decisions.consequence: GameScreen owns final panel state; provider calls remain behind RankingService and appProviders.

decisions.decision: RankingScreen renders top 10 and user best using useRanking/RankingService only, with no provider imports.
decisions.reason: Ranking UI must remain replaceable across Firebase, API, SQL or backend providers.
decisions.consequence: Provider-specific ranking behavior remains behind RankingPort; UI handles loading, empty and error states generically.

decisions.decision: Use client-create-only Firestore rules for users/{uid} and gameResults/{resultId}, with ranking reads restricted to authenticated users.
decisions.reason: The MVP needs ranking persistence while preventing cross-user writes, result mutation, and client deletes.
decisions.consequence: Rules validate result shape and positive durations, but frontend timer remains manipulable until server-side validation is added.

decisions.decision: Document provider replacement through docs/REPLACE_PROVIDERS.md and require provider swaps through appProviders.ts only.
decisions.reason: Firebase must remain replaceable without touching Home, Login, Game, Result or Ranking screens.
decisions.consequence: Future auth/ranking migrations must implement AuthPort/RankingPort adapters and run provider-leak checks plus typecheck.

decisions.title: Validated Supernova MVP QA baseline
decisions.decision: QA validation uses typecheck, architecture grep checks, JSON validation, Playwright mock-provider flow, and a manual checklist until a test framework is added.
decisions.rationale: The project has no Vitest/Jest setup and AGENTS forbids builds after changes.

decisions.title: Closed final QA triage debts
decisions.decision: Final QA triage fixed feedback cleanup, result save-once consistency, provider env validation, and invalid merge IDs.
decisions.rationale: The final QA requested closing concrete bugs and architecture debt before adding new features.

decisions.title: Prepared internal demo package
decisions.decision: The first internal demo uses README onboarding, docs/DEMO_SCRIPT.md, .env.example provider comments, build validation, and mock-provider responsive checks.
decisions.rationale: The user requested a clean internal demo without adding new features.

decisions.decision: Use a Node/Express backend as the authoritative source for game session timing and result writes.
decisions.reason: Frontend timers can be manipulated and should only be visual for ranking integrity.
decisions.consequence: GameScreen starts/finishes sessions through GameSessionService; backend calculates durationMs and writes gameResults with Firebase Admin SDK.

decisions.decision: Use ApiRankingProvider and ApiGameSessionProvider by default with Firebase Auth tokens.
decisions.reason: Firebase remains Hosting/Auth/Firestore while writes move behind the backend API.
decisions.consequence: VITE_RANKING_PROVIDER=api and VITE_GAME_SESSION_PROVIDER=api are the production-like defaults; mocks remain available for local demos.

decisions.decision: Block direct frontend writes to gameResults and gameSessions in Firestore rules.
decisions.reason: Only the backend should create sessions and ranking results.
decisions.consequence: Firestore client SDK can read allowed data but cannot persist ranking results directly; Admin SDK writes bypass rules.
